Screening, Risk Reduction Strategies, and Clinical Management of Unaffected Carriers of BRCA1 or BRCA2 Pathogenic Variants

evidence_entities.text: Breast cancer remains the most frequently diagnosed malignancy in women worldwide. In 2022, almost 2.3 million cases were diagnosed, with an estimated death toll of over 666,000
approximately 5-15% of cases linked to pathogenic variants primarily in BRCA1 or BRCA2
Annual magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) remains the most sensitive screening tool for early breast cancer detection in high-risk individuals
Selective estrogen receptor modulators (SERMs) and aromatase inhibitors have shown potential as chemopreventive agents
Risk-reducing surgeries, such as bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (BSO) and mastectomy, significantly lower the risk of breast and ovarian cancer
Carriers of pathogenic BRCA2 variants have a lifetime risk of developing breast cancer ranging from 38% to 84%, while the risk of developing ovarian cancer ranges from 16.5% to 27.0%
Carriers of BRCA1 mutation may have higher risk of both breast and ovarian cancers
